title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Runtime Contract: Handler Interface Contract
summary: Handlers construct database pools and loggers per request, middleware changes response order, mutable globals leak across tests, and business rules cannot run without HTTP fixtures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo handler interface contract increment in composed handler and dependency boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Handler Interface Contract · Theory Handler Interface Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1122 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Handler Interface Contract contract for the supplied data.
